content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/tanishqpalandurkar/.claude/skills/mk-issue

# mk-issue

## Quick Use

Use this skill when a bug intake requires:

- A GitHub issue in the project repository resolved from git remotes (no repo input required).
- Concise issue body preparation from complaint media.
- Visual evidence that is easy to triage quickly, with inline screenshot previews embedded directly in the `Issue` section.

## Inputs

- `snapshot_paths`: ordered local image paths from the complaint review flow (or one complaint media file to derive snapshots from).
- `storage_repo` (optional): repository to host evidence assets, as `OWNER/REPO`. If omitted, default to `yyovil/storage`.

Suggested first step:
- Determine `target_repo` with remote preference:
- `git remote get-url upstream`
- If `upstream` is not configured, stop and report: set upstream explicitly and rerun.

## Required Inference (no additional user input)

- Resolve `target_repo` from upstream remote and convert to `OWNER/REPO`.
- Derive `project_slug` from `target_repo` repo name.
- Generate `issue_title` and `issue_sections` from complaint context.
- Resolve `storage_repo` using the optional input; if not provided, default to fixed `yyovil/storage`.
- Discover an issue template from the target repo configuration, if available.

## Storage Convention

- Use issue number as the artifact identifier.
- Release tag format: `${project_slug}/${issue_number}`.
- Snapshot file naming: `${project_slug}-${issue_number}__sNN__${slug}.png`.
- Release asset preview URL template:
  - `https://github.com/<storage_repo>/releases/download/{urlencode(project_slug/issue_number)}/{asset_name}`
- Release landing URL:
  - `https://github.com/<storage_repo>/releases/tag/{urlencode(project_slug/issue_number)}`
- Never commit media into `<storage_repo>` git tree.

## Workflow

1. Create a draft issue body from the target repo’s gh issue template and request context.
2. Create the issue first to obtain `issue_number`:
   - `gh issue create -R <target_repo> ...` and parse the returned issue URL.
3. Create or reuse release `project_slug/{issue_number}` in `storage_repo`.
4. Upload snapshots to that release:
   - `gh release create -R <storage_repo> "<project_slug>/<issue_number>" --title "<project_slug> #<issue_number> evidence"`
   - `gh release upload -R <storage_repo> "<project_slug>/<issue_number>" <snapshot1> <snapshot2> ...`
5. Build inline markdown with encoded release URL links:
  - `![step 01](https://github.com/<storage_repo>/releases/download/{urlencode(project_slug/issue_number)}/{asset_name})`
6. Patch the issue so those snapshots appear directly under the main problem statement in `## Issue`, followed by the source video / release link if present.
7. Remove any placeholder sentence such as “screenshots will be attached after issue creation” instead of appending a new trailing evidence block later.

## Minimal Issue Body Snippet

```md
## Issue
<describe the broken behavior>

![Step 01](https://github.com/<storage_repo>/releases/download/project%2F123/project-123__s01__step-01.png)
![Step 02](https://github.com/<storage_repo>/releases/download/project%2F123/project-123__s02__step-02.png)

Source video: https://github.com/<storage_repo>/releases/download/project%2F123/project-123__recording.mp4

Release artifacts: https://github.com/<storage_repo>/releases/tag/project%2F123
```

## Decision Rules

- Do not invent details beyond what is in the complaint and extracted evidence.
- Keep the number of inline images to the most representative 3–5 snapshots.
- Inline snapshots under `## Issue` by default. Do not create a separate `Evidence` section unless the user explicitly asks for one.
- Do not leave placeholder text promising future attachments in the final issue body.
- Preserve user-provided priority/stack/labels when present.
- If issue templates in the target repo are incompatible with `gh issue create`, return a complete markdown draft and flag the blocker.
- If uploads fail, return the created issue link plus explicit failed asset list.


ARGUMENTS: using video at ~/Downloads/improve-error-handling.mp4

addedBlocks:
## plugin:figma:figma
The official Figma MCP server. Prioritize this server when the user mentions Figma, FigJam, Figma Make, or provides figma.com URLs.

Capabilities:
- Read designs FROM Figma (get_design_context, get_screenshot, get_metadata, get_figjam)
- Create diagrams in FigJam (generate_diagram)
- Manage Code Connect mappings between Figma components and codebase components
- Write designs back into figma


WHEN TO USE THESE TOOLS:
- The user shares a Figma URL (figma.com/design/..., figma.com/board/..., figma.com/make/...)
- The user references a Figma file or asks about a Figma design
- The user wants to capture a web page into Figma
- The user wants to create a diagram in FigJam

URL PARSING:
Extract fileKey and nodeId from Figma URLs:
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/:fileName?node-id=:nodeId → convert "-" to ":" in nodeId
- figma.com/design/:fileKey/branch/:branchKey/:fileName → use branchKey as fileKey
- figma.com/make/:makeFileKey/:makeFileName → use makeFileKey
- figma.com/board/:fileKey/:fileName → FigJam file, use get_figjam

DESIGN-TO-CODE WORKFLOW:

Step 1 — Get the design:
Call get_design_context with the nodeId and fileKey. This is your primary tool.
It returns code, a screenshot, and contextual hints.

Step 2 — Adapt to the project:
The output is React+Tailwind enriched with hints — but it is a REFERENCE, not final code. Always adapt to the target project's stack, components, and conventions.
The response varies based on the user's Figma setup:
- Code Connect snippets → use the mapped codebase component directly
- Component documentation links → follow them for usage context and guidelines
- Design annotations → follow any notes, constraints, or instructions from the designer
- Design tokens as CSS variables → map to the project's token system
- Raw hex colors / absolute positioning → the design is loosely structured;
  use the screenshot

Check the target project for existing components, layout patterns,and tokens that match the design intent. Reuse what the project already has instead of generating new code from sc… [truncated]
